title: Isolate prokaryotes from sponge tissue (SAP)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"90sla","text":"Protocoll to create a fixed suspension of sponge associated prokaryotes (SAP) from sponge tissue.This can serve as the basis for Fluorescence in situ hybridisation and/or cell sorting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":71},{"style":"italic","offset":142,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6154c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7erh","text":"This protocol was tested for different sponge species but might also be adapted to other organisms (let others know)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dmq09","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"69jbi","text":"The protocol was modified from :","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ep2ar","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7i7o3","text":"Fieseler L, Horn M, Wagner M, Hentschel U. (2006) Discovery of the novel candidate phylum \"Potibactetia\" in marine sponges (vol 70, pg 3724, 2004). Applied and Environmental Microbiology;72(8):5677-.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e1qtj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9a52f","text":"PMID:15184179 DOI:10.1128/AEM.70.6.3724-3732.2004","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":18,"length":31}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E96B3E974E8E4FBB964F290CAD5A0715","url":"https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.70.6.3724-3732.2004"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2b3of","text":"Prepare CMASW:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c4jks","text":"400mM NaCl, 27.6mM Na2SO4, 2.3mM NaHCO3, 8.9mM KCl, 0.8mM KBr, 0.4 mM H3BO3, 0.15mM SrCl2, 0.07mM NaF in MilliQ and autoclave. \u003e\u003e(This likely will also work with NaCl only but adjust salinity)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":21,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":24,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":38,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":71,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":74,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":88,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"91gf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"34jdg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mince sponge tissue in ice cold CMASW using a razor blade within a petry dish on ice Tissue can be either fresh or thawed from -20°C samples Process about the tissue volume that would fit in a 1.5 ml eppendorf tube
Squeeze remaining pieces using 15 ml falcon tube lid °C, on ice
Transfer to fresh 50ml Falcon tube and add up to 20 mL, CMASW
Filter through 40µm cell strainer into a fresh 50 ml Falcon tube Corning® 40µm Cell Strainer Corning Catalog #431750
Transfer to fresh 50 ml falcon tube and add up to 50 mL, CMASW
Spin down at 600g to pellet cells for 00:10:00 at 4 °C
Transfer supernatant to fresh 50ml falcon tube and add up to 50 mL, CMASW The supernatant should contain the bacterial fraction
Spin down at 1600g to pellet cells for 00:10:00 at 4 °C and resuspend in 50 mL, CMASW
Repeat step 10 until solution becomes clear In case of sponge tissue this gets rid of secondary metbolites that inhibit downstream applications.
If solution is clear resuspend pellet into 1ml ice cold 1 Mass Percent, PFA in CMASW and transfer to fresh 2ml tube 4% PFA for fixation by Martin Thomas Jahn
Fix overnight 4 °C, in fridge Fixation time and fixative concentration might be optimised for different cell types find basic guidelines here: Silva protocols
Pellet cells by centrifugation ( 00:10:00, at 4000 x g ) discharge supernatant
Thoroughly resuspend fixed cells in 500 µl PBS
Add 500 µl absolute ethanol and resuspend cells thoroughly
At this stage the cell suspension can be stored at -20 °C for several months
creator: Martin Thomas Jahn
